content.problemStatement:
You are given two matrices AAA and BBB, each with RRR rows (numbered 111 through RRR) and CCC columns (numbered 111 through CCC). Let's denote an element of AAA or BBB in row iii and column jjj by Ai,jA_{i,j}Ai,j​ or Bi,jB_{i,j}Bi,j​ respectively.

You are also given an integer XXX. You may perform the following operation on AAA any number of times:


Choose an integer vvv.
Choose XXX consecutive elements of AAA, either in the same row or in the same column.
Add vvv to each of the chosen elements of AAA.
Determine whether it is possible to change AAA to BBB in a finite number of operations.
constraints: the sum of RRR over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103
the sum of CCC over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers RRR, CCC and XXX.
RRR lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains CCC space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,…,Ai,CA_{i,1}, A_{i,2}, \ldots, A_{i,C}Ai,1​,Ai,2​,…,Ai,C​.
RRR more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains CCC space-separated integers Bi,1,Bi,2,…,Bi,CB_{i,1}, B_{i,2}, \ldots, B_{i,C}Bi,1​,Bi,2​,…,Bi,C​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"Yes"` if there is a sequence of operations that changes the matrix AAA to BBB, or `"No"` if such a sequence of operations does not exist.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can add −1-1−1 to both elements in row 111 and add −1-1−1 to both elements in column 222.
Example case 2: After any operation, the sum of elements of AAA remains even. However, the sum of elements of BBB is odd, so AAA cannot be changed to BBB.
